## Avian influenza A(H7N9)
The last H7N9 case was  reported in February 2018 and no new cases have been reported since [Figure  1].  In total, 3 human cases of H7N9 have  been reported to the World Health Organization (WHO) in wave 6 (October 2017 –  September 2018). Cases in the 6th wave occurred in 3 out of 34 administrative  regions across China [Figure 2], a drop from the 12-29 regions that reported  cases in waves 1-5. This decrease in cases may be attributable to China's new  vaccine against both H5 and H7 in poultry, which was deployed after the number  of illnesses caused in the 5th wave. A total of 1567 cases including at least  613 deaths have been reported globally since 2013. Two travel-related cases  were reported in Canada in January 2015.
Figure 1.  Temporal Distribution of Avian Influenza  A(H7N9) in China, June 25, 2016 – September 30, 2018.
Note: Graph  was prepared by the Centre for Immunization and Respiratory Infectious Diseases  (CIRID) using data from the latest WHO Monthly Influenza at the Human-Animal  Interface Risk Assessment. This graph reflects data available through these  risk assessments as of September 30, 2018.
Month and year of symptom onset or earliest report date
Figure 2. Spatial Distribution of Avian Influenza A(H7N9)  in China, Wave 6,  October 1, 2017 to September  30, 2018.
Note: Map  was prepared by the Centre for Immunization and Respiratory Infectious Diseases  (CIRID) using data from the latest WHO Monthly Influenza at the Human-Animal  Interface Risk Assessment. This map  reflects data available through these risk assessments as of September 30, 2018.
The spatial distribution of reported avian influenza A(H7N9) cases in wave 6 from October 1, 2017 to September 30, 2018 has been displayed in Figure 2. Throughout October 1, 2017 to September 30, 2018, a total of 3 cases have been reported. The 3 cases in wave 6 were spread across China in Guangdong Province, Xinjiang Uyghur Autonomous Region, and Yunnan Province.

## Avian Influenza A(H7N4)
On February 14, 2018, the WHO was notified of the first  known human case of Avian Influenza A(H7N4). No new cases have been reported  since.

## Avian Influenza A(H9N2)
In September 2018, no new cases of H9N2 were reported  to the WHO. A total of 45 cases, including 1 death, have been reported globally  since 1998. No cases have been reported from Canada.

## Avian Influenza A(H5N1)
In September 2018, no new cases of H5N1 were reported to the  WHO. A total of 860 cases including 454 deaths have been reported globally  since 1997. One fatal travel-related case of A(H5N1) was reported in Canada in  January 2014.

## Avian Influenza A(H5N6)
In September 2018, one new case of H5N6 was reported to the  WHO. The case is a 22 year old male from Guangdong Province in China who  developed symptoms and was hospitalized after contact with poultry. There have  been a total of 21 cases, including 14 deaths, reported globally since 2014,  with all cases occurring in China.

## Swine origin Influenza A(H3N2)v
In September  2018, no new cases of H3N2v were reported to the WHO. The Indiana State  Department of Health reported the first case of H3N2v this year in the United  States in a county fairgoer who had been in close proximity to swine. The case  is a child (<12 years old) who developed symptoms on June 18 and has since  fully recovered. One locally-acquired case of H3N2v was reported in Canada in  December 2016.

## Swine origin Influenza A(H1N2)v
In September  2018, no new cases of H1N2v were reported to the WHO.  Since 2005, there have been a total of 26  confirmed cases of H1N2v in the United States. Most of the reported cases  resulted in mild illness.

## Swine origin Influenza A(H1N1)v
In September  2018, no new cases of H1N1v were reported to the WHO. There have been a total  of 22 cases reported since 2005 and only one case reported in 2018.

## Middle East Respiratory Syndrome - Coronavirus (MERS-CoV)
From September 1st 2018 to September 30th  2018, 14 new cases of MERS-CoV, of which 6 died, were reported by the Saudi  Arabian Ministry of Health and the WHO [Figure 3], with most cases (13/14)  originating from Saudi Arabia and one being reported by South Korea [Figure 4].  The South Korean case was a travel-associated case of a South Korean national  who travelled to Kuwait for business before flying back to South Korea via  Dubai. Of the cases reported in September 2018, 13/14 (92.9%) were male and the  median age was 59 years old (age range of 34-66 years old). A total of 2260  laboratory-confirmed cases of MERS-CoV cases, including 803 deaths, have been  reported globally since 2012.  No cases  have been reported in Canada.
Figure 3. Global Count of Human  Cases of MERS-CoV Reported to the WHO, January 1, 2017 – September 30, 2018.
Note: Graph  was prepared by the Centre for Immunization and Respiratory Infectious Diseases  (CIRID) using data from the WHO Disease Outbreak News and Saudi Arabia's  Ministry of Health. This graph reflects data available as of September 30, 2018.
Month of symptom onset or earliest report date
Figure 4. Spatial Distribution of  MERS-CoV, January 1, 2018 to September 30, 2018.
Note: Map  was prepared by the Centre for Immunization and Respiratory Infectious Diseases  (CIRID) using data from the latest WHO Monthly Influenza at the Human-Animal  Interface Risk Assessment. This map reflects data available through these risk  assessments as of September 30, 2018.
The spatial distribution of reported MERS cases from January 1, 2018 to September 30, 2018 has been displayed in Figure 4. Throughout January 1, 2018 to September 30, 2018, the greatest number of cases have always been reported from Saudi Arabia. In 2018, up until September 30, Saudi Arabia has reported 58-679 cases so far.
